observations: Lines 3-11 open with one dense descriptive paragraph, then snap to the one-line turn at line 11; the emphasis rule is earned rather than ornamental.
Lines 21-35 keep the gate confrontation in clipped declaratives and short exchanges, matching the guide's competence-collision rhythm.
Lines 63-89 let Alexei's smoother evasions run longer, then Jaina cuts back to short questions; power and cadence track each other cleanly.
Lines 365-377 expand into Jaina's mechanism read, then puncture the run with the stand-alone line at 369: 'Resonance.'
Lines 547-579 use one longer stair-description sentence at 551, then tighten the close into short, pressured beats that keep forward drag.
Lines 31-33 keep Jaina's open-pressure language plain and usable: 'ask about his grain' stays scene-native instead of bureaucratic.
Lines 77-87 preserve Alexei's aristocratic evasiveness without sliding into memo-speak; his register sounds practiced, not administrative.
Line 111 originally used planner-like wording ('reclaiming one lane'); it was tightened to 'retaking the conversation' to keep the prose inside live social pressure.
Line 227 originally summarized the tour in process language; it now reads as Jaina noticing 'chosen rooms,' which is concrete and POV-bound.
Line 459 originally said 'The model is elegant'; it now says 'The design is elegant,' which stays technical without sounding like a paper abstract.
Line 467 originally used the more abstract 'threshold of response'; it now lands on 'answering point,' which fits Jaina's embodied magical logic better.

fixes.before: "The grain," Alexei said, reclaiming one lane with practiced smoothness, "is my burden."
fixes.after: "The grain," Alexei said, retaking the conversation with practiced smoothness, "is my burden."

fixes.before: They crossed two more corridors and a stair. By the second turn Jaina knew she was being shown a path rather than a place.
fixes.after: They crossed two more corridors and a stair. By the second turn Jaina knew Jandice was walking her through chosen rooms.

fixes.before: "I'm not pretending anything. The model is elegant."
fixes.after: "I'm not pretending anything. The design is elegant."

fixes.before: "You're treating death as a threshold of response," Jaina said.
fixes.after: "You're treating death as the answering point," Jaina said.

observations: Line 5 uses 'patient logic of harvest work' once and moves on; the image comes from labor and scale, not decorative fantasy blur.
Lines 160-180 keep the lake and ward imagery tactile and infrastructural; the ward 'taste' and pressure suit Jaina's magical sensory lane.
Line 209's notation 'hit Jaina squarely in the chest' is one clean body-pressure image at a recognition point, not a stacked flourish.
Line 305's 'worse than rot' sharpens institutional horror in a single stroke and does not trigger a second competing image.
Line 563's pressure 'like weather against the face' stays within the guide's approved pressure/weather source domain.
Most paragraphs remain 1-4 sentences, which matches the guide; the chapter's visual shape is mostly single-sentence or two-sentence pressure blocks.
Lines 5-11 give one heavier opener paragraph and then immediately return to a one-line action turn at 11, satisfying scene-balance guidance.
Lines 147-180 break the lake crossing into compact movement units rather than one descriptive slab; motion keeps interrupting description.
Lines 227-245 allow one denser archive paragraph, then line 247 returns straight to dialogue and live reaction.
Lines 551-579 let the descent take one earned longer paragraph, then shorten toward the cliff-edge close instead of ending on summary.
Explicit no-frills tags dominate the tagged lines: 57 uses of 'said' and 8 uses of 'asked' versus only 3 stronger tags, which stays within the anti-creative-tag rule.
Lines 19-35 use a clean two-speaker run with minimal tagging; clarity holds without decorative attributions.
Lines 69-89 keep Alexei and Jaina legible with sparse no-frills tags and social posture doing most of the work.
Lines 309-337 rely on alternating short lines and action beats instead of tag variety; the exchange remains easy to track.

Global dialogue-to-narration ratio measures at roughly 31:69 (1435 dialogue words to 3172 narration words), below the 45:55 overall target but still partly explained by the chapter's travel, archive, and dread-heavy sections.
Lines 5-9 prioritize magical structure and exposure before social texture; Jaina reads wards, distance, and risk before she reads courtesy.
Lines 160-180 keep her sensory signature on teeth, ward taste, pressure, and lattice behavior rather than scenic awe.
Lines 209-245 lock into her scholar-pattern filter: notation, lineage, method, and the smell of Dalaran over corruption.
Lines 365-375 show classic Jaina thought progression: observed pattern -> hypothesis gap -> privacy ward -> risky contact with Antonidas.
Lines 391-409 keep her full-speed mage register for Antonidas alone, which matches the voice-sheet audience calibration.

fixes.before: Her stomach tightened instead. The master below had already decided.

observations: Likely adverbs per page come out to about 1.63, well under the floor of 4 per page.
Passive-voice candidates measure at roughly 3.92% of sentences, which stays just inside the guide's 4% ceiling.
Real simile load stays low: one substantive 'like' comparison at line 563, with no 'as if' constructions in chapter prose.
Filter-word use is limited and usually tied to social observation rather than generic distance; the chapter does not drown in saw/felt/heard scaffolding.
Showing remains dominant in the hall, archive, call, and descent sections; conclusions usually arrive through posture, material detail, and ward behavior first.
Exposition integration passes: lines 160-190 and 243-245 embed worldbuilding in Jaina's immediate read of wards, infrastructure, and research rather than pausing for encyclopedia narration.
Power dynamics pass cleanly in the gate and hall scenes: lines 21-35 and 51-89 give Jaina short pressure lines while Alexei answers with smoother longer evasions.
Scene shape tracks the guide: the chapter opens with a problem already in motion, pivots hard at the handwriting mirror in lines 209-245, and resolves on cliff-edge forward pressure at 547-579.
Speculative domestication is strong throughout; wards, resonance, fungi, and necromantic process all read as working realities, not spectacle.
Subtext density is strongest in the Alexei/Jandice social fencing at lines 51-145, where courtesy is status-testing rather than plain disclosure.

Subtext density weakens in the theory duel at lines 457-499, where the argument becomes more explicit than the confrontation target would ideally allow; that was flagged but not expanded or re-concealed because adding new material would violate the no-new-content rule.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — lines 5-9, 160-180, and 391-409 keep precise arcane diction without modern lab jargon; the few flatter abstractions at 459 and 467 were corrected.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — clipped questions and short judgments dominate pressure scenes, while longer reasoning chains appear only in the archive, sending-stone call, and mechanism talk.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — about 3.04 metaphorical images per thousand words, grounded in weather, structure, pressure, and craft rather than ornate fantasy abstraction.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — guilt and stress surface through over-focus, speed, stillness, and bodily tension at 371-375, 437-439, and 537 instead of open self-labeling.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — she moves from observation to pattern recognition to hypothesis to objection at 243-245, 365-375, and 459-489.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — teeth, ward taste, pressure, light, resonance, and the bodily cost of magical attention dominate her sensory lane.

ruleViolations: Global dialogue ratio remains low for the chapter's total mix (31:69); not fixed because solving it would require adding dialogue rather than tightening existing prose.
Lines 457-499 run more explicit than the confrontation subtext target ideally wants, but the exchange still sounds like Jaina and Jandice rather than an auditor's rewrite.
